lessons: Added non-working residents + non-work (passive) income contribution rates (266 minimum, 3,442 exemption, 6.92%/7% NI + 5.17% health). Data was NOT in the skill, sourced live from btl.gov.il (NotWorking + non-working rates pages) and kolzchut exemptions page.

Expert review caught 3 CRITICALs on the first draft: (1) non-working spouse pays 0 not 266 (Form 673, akeret bayit), (2) the 3,442 passive exemption does not stack on work income, (3) pensioners/retirement-age are exempt from the NI portion. All three fixed by scoping the base table and adding an Exemptions and exceptions override table.

Legacy backfill: built evidence.json from scratch (118 entries, 178 facts) for the whole BL benefits catalog. Benefit amounts sourced to the 2026 rate circular; some scattered amounts (1,752.33 maternity, 1,314.25 work-injury, income-support tiers) cited to their benefit pages but not individually re-fetched, only the changed contributions facts were Judge-verified verbatim.

persistent_fail / CARRY: the benefit-amount evidence entries for the non-changed legacy catalog should be Judge-verified against their benefit pages in a future cycle (this cycle verified only the changed contributions section + spot-checks).

Added employee rate table BY age and pension status (under-18=0, working pensioner=0, 67-70 non-pensioner=3.93/10.03, woman ret-67=3.95/10.24, disability=3.23/5.17), self-employed age-varies note, te'um (multiple-employer) coordination rule, and household-help employer track. All employee rates verified by rendering the official btl.gov.il employee rate table via Playwright (WebFetch garbled it across 3 tries). Self-employed-pensioner exact % and household-help exact % ROUTED to btl pages (not fabricated).

ROOT-CAUSE of why v1.4.0 (and all prior gates) missed the age brackets: the pipeline verifies stated values + audits coverage against a topic-checklist, but never enumerated the official rate table's full row set. Fixed in update-skill (new Phase 3g.1 Rate-Table Completeness Pass) + admin-creator (Phase 1.7 Step C.1) on 2026-06-13.
CARRY/minor: chofesh-lelo-tashlum (unpaid-leave employer-continues) rule not explicitly named — non-material edge, revisit if a user hits it.
